cpt.description: Emergency department visit, high medical decision complexity

carc.description: Non-covered service — payer determined service not medically necessary.
carc.appealNotes: Frequently overturned when admitting diagnosis clinically supports the service. Cite ACEP or specialty guidelines. ER visits for infectious, cardiac, or neurologic diagnoses are almost always upheld. Request peer-to-peer review between your physician and the reviewing physician.

ADJUSTMENT CODES

CO-4 The procedure code is inconsistent with the modifier used or a required modifier is missing.

N130 Consult plan benefit documents for information about restrictions for this service.
MA130 Your claim contains incomplete and/or invalid information.

YOUR APPEAL RIGHTS
You may file an internal appeal within 180 days of the denial date.

patientFacingSummary: Your insurer denied $2127 that may be wrongful — you have 2 appealable denials with a strong chance of reversal. We've drafted a ready-to-send appeal letter to help you recover this amount.
appealLetter:
**Jane Q. Smith**
1234 Member Address
City, IL 60601
(555) 123-4567

**Date:** [Current Date]

**BlueCross BlueShield of Illinois**
Appeals and Grievances Department
[Insurer Address]
Chicago, IL 60601

**RE: Level-1 Internal Appeal – Claim ID #48291**
Member ID: BCB-2204819334
Date of Service: June 14, 2026
Procedure Code: 99285

Dear Appeals Committee:

I am writing to formally appeal the denial of the above-referenced claim for emergency department services rendered at Memorial Hospital. The claim was denied under code **CO-50 (Non-covered service — payer determined service not medically necessary)**. I respectfully disagree with this determination.

**Clinical Justification for Medical Necessity**

I presented to the emergency department with symptoms subsequently diagnosed as **pneumonia (ICD-10: J18.9)**. Pneumonia is a potentially life-threatening respiratory infection that requires urgent evaluation to assess severity, rule out sepsis, and determine appropriate treatment. The American College of Emergency Physicians and clinical guidelines recognize that pneumonia presenting with acute symptoms warrants emergency evaluation, as delayed treatment significantly increases morbidity and mortality risk. A high-complexity medical decision (99285) was appropriate given the need for diagnostic workup, clinical assessment, and treatment initiation.

**Request for Reviewer Information**

Pursuant to my rights under **ERISA §503** and applicable Illinois insurance regulations, I formally request disclosure of the following regarding the physician who reviewed and denied this claim: (1) full name and professional title; (2) medical license number and state(s) of licensure; (3) board certifications and specialty area(s); and (4) confirmation of whether they personally reviewed my complete medical record. A "medical necessity" determination requires review by a qualified peer physician. If the reviewer lacks relevant specialty credentials, this constitutes additional grounds for appeal.

**Request for Reconsideration**

I respectfully request a Level-1 reconsideration of this claim within 30 days. Please contact me if additional documentation is required.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Jane Q. Smith
